programme.title: TikTok Star Murder Trial
programme.desc: Police said the defendant, Ali Abulaban, called his mother after the shooting deaths of his wife Ana and Rayburn Barron. Jurors later learned about the day his wife, Ana, had enough of the alleged abuse. But just three days after Ali left and went to stay in a hotel, Ana was killed along with her friend, Rayburn Barron. Displayed for the courtroom to follow were the arguments, fights, and disparaging comments between Ali and Ana days before the double homicide occurred.

programme.title: TikTok Star Murder Trial
programme.desc: Ali Abulaban's lawyers had questions for the Detective-Sergeant who interviewed the defendant after his arrest. Police concluded that the long-standing abuse of his wife, Ana, ultimately led to her murder along with that of her friend, Rayburn Barron. Narcissistic, impulsive, and theatrical. This was the demeanor of Ali Abulaban during his first interview with police, according to Detective Robert Hopper.

programme.title: TikTok Star Murder Trial
programme.desc: Jurors heard the perspective of police on what happened to the defendant's wife Ana, and her friend, Rayburn Barron. According to Detective Robert Hopper, jealousy and anger had been on Ali Abulaban's mind before he committed these murders. But, according to Hopper, there was no suspicion of a psychological issue or a mental health disorder.

programme.title: TikTok Star Murder Trial
programme.desc: The jury learned more about the gun used in the shooting deaths of his wife, Ana, and her friend, Rayburn Barron. They were shot at close range in her apartment. At the time of the fatal shootings, the defendant claimed he had a license to carry a Glock pistol. This would be the game used to Murder his wife and her friend in their apartment. On the stand was the firearm expert who examined the Glock 19 semi-automatic gun and ran some test-fires.

programme.title: TikTok Star Murder Trial
programme.desc: The medical examiner who performed the autopsies on both Ana Abulaban and Rayburn Barron testified. Jurors saw graphic photos from the examination as they learned more about the gunshot wounds. According to the medical examiner, both victims were shot at close range with the shooter being a few inches from their heads. Jurors would have to weigh those details when deciding whether Ali Abulaban shot them in a heat of passion or as a murder plot carried out.

programme.title: TikTok Star Murder Trial
programme.sub-title: From the Autopsy to Lead Investigator
programme.desc: Things get emotional for Ali Abulaban when the autopsy photos of his wife, Ana, are displayed.

programme.title: TikTok Star Murder Trial
programme.desc: Investigators treated these murders as a domestic violence case where the husband allegedly used coercion, intimidation, and threats to control his wife until it finally ended in a deadly encounter. Lead Detective Ricardo Escalante explained to jurors how his team began reviewing important Crime Scene evidence. Jurors learned of his erratic behavior in the days leading up to the murders.

programme.title: TikTok Star Murder Trial
programme.desc: Prosecutors presented images of a trashed apartment where Ana and Ali Abulaban once lived together with their daughter. On the stand in this murder trial was lead Detective Ricardo Escalante who explained to the jury how this domestic abuse case turned deadly. Jurors' undivided attention turned to the bloody crime scene photos. According to witnesses who testified, Ali tried to prevent his wife from seeing anyone while they were separated.

programme.title: TikTok Star Murder Trial
programme.desc: Police saw bullet holes in the wall, projectiles on the floor and blood spatter everywhere when they arrived at the crime scene where Ana Abulaban and Rayburn Barron lost their lives. Detective Ricardo Escalante claimed the defendant plotted and carried out the two murders. He was accused of first ransacking the apartment, setting up a secret listening device to spy on Ana, and later showing up to the apartment with a loaded gun—all goal-oriented behavior.

programme.title: TikTok Star Murder Trial
programme.desc: A domestic violence case. That's how the lead case agent summarized the shooting deaths of Ana Abulaban and her friend, Rayburn Barron. Jurors learned from Detective Ricardo Escalante's testimony more about what was on his mind in the lead up to the murders. The drama between Ali and Ana Abulaban through social media messages and emails were on display in the courtroom.

programme.title: TikTok Star Murder Trial
programme.desc: Social media messages and posts dominated trial testimony. The lead Detective on the stand put together a timeline of events showing details about Ali Abulaban's behavior and mindset. The State claimed this led him to kill his wife, Ana, and her friend, Rayburn Barron. Detective Ricardo Escalante was not done presenting all the evidence they retrieved in the double murders. He was testifying for the second day in a row.

programme.title: TikTok Star Murder Trial
programme.desc: Jurors hear disparaging voicemail messages that are played in court.

programme.title: TikTok Star Murder Trial
programme.desc: Incriminating online searches law enforcement retrieved included: How to plant a listening device, Decapitated Head, and Chopped Bodies. The prosecution presented a story of domestic abuse in the marriage of the defendant and his wife, Ana. This was done through a timeline of social media and text messages. Prosecutors wanted to show how this alleged abuse went so far that it led to the murders of two innocent young People.

programme.title: TikTok Star Murder Trial
programme.desc: Jurors learned about the social media persona that the followers of Ali Abulaban knew. Before he stood trial for the death of his wife Ana and Rayburn Barron, he was known as Jinn-Kid and was a rising online star. Later, the jury heard even more audio recordings of the fights between Ali and Ana Abulaban. Detective Ricardo Escalante presented the Glock-19, the murder weapon, up close to the jury.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: In 1979, 16-year-old Kim Bryant disappears from outside a Dairy Queen in Las Vegas. Three weeks later, her body is found: she had been sexually assaulted and bludgeoned to death. 40 years later, genetic genealogy finally names the killer.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: Mark Dribin vanishes from Portland in 1999. After over 20 years, cutting-edge DNA technology finally unmasks his killer - and unearths even darker secrets.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: In 1987, the body of Darlene Krashoc is discovered behind a Colorado Springs restaurant. She had been savagely beaten, raped and strangled. Investigators struggle to identify this barbaric killer. 30 years later, science provides a breakthrough.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives
programme.desc: "Ice Cold Murder" is the story of the dedicated investigation into Christy Mirack's murder and how the Bloodline Detectives traced her killer with DNA.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: A young couple is murdered in Montana in 1956. In 2019, investigators use voluntary genealogical databases to catch the killer.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: In 1964 Hazleton, Pennsylvania, the brutalized body of 9-year-old Marise Chiverella is discovered after she disappears walking to school. Half a century later, a teen prodigy in investigative genetic genealogy helps detectives solve this crime.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.sub-title: The Murder Mystery of Mary London
programme.desc: In 1981 a young woman is found murdered on the side of the road. Who killed her remains a mystery for decades until law enforcement can utilize genetic genealogy to crack the case.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: Thirty years after college student Cathy Sposito is brutally killed on a hiking trail in Prescott, Arizona, can a groundbreaking forensic tool finally identify her killer?

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: In 1985, Terri McAdams is murdered in her fiance's apartment. In 2023, DNA and genealogy identify the killer.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: Police are stumped when a toddler's body is found in Escatawpa River, leading to a decades-long mystery.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives
programme.desc: This is the story of Lynette White's senseless killing and how a ground-breaking DNA method solved the crime.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.sub-title: The Hair That Called His Bluff
programme.desc: A single strand of hair, a DNA match and decades of detective work finally solve the murder of schoolgirl Rashell Ward after over 40 years.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives
programme.desc: This case follows the investigation into the Halling Rapist and how familial DNA helped solve the crime.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: A 65-year-old mystery is solved when DNA testing identifies the remains of 7-year-old Chester Alfred Breiney.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: In 1976, 16-year-old Pamela Maurer leaves a friend's house to walk to a nearby location for a soft drink. She never makes it home. The next morning, road workers discover her body along College Road. New DNA technology solves the case in 2019.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: Valentine's Day, 1974. Carla Walker is raped and killed, leading to a cold case that takes 46 years to crack.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: Can the groundbreaking tool of investigative genetic genealogy solve the tragic 1993 murder of Melissa Lee, which leaves detectives baffled in Bothell, Washington?

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: A string of rapes in the mid-2000s shrouds the community of Fayetteville, North Carolina in fear. Years later, a forensic DNA technique brings the twisted perpetrator to justice.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: In 1996, Danielle Houchins is found murdered. DNA technology finally unmasks the killer in the 2010s.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: In 1996, a woman takes her life in a Virginia cemetery and leaves a note that she wishes to remain unidentified.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In the summer of 1999, a brutal serial killer who traveled by train terrorized Texas. The FBI assembled a special task force to profile the violent drifter and help locate him.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A series of brutal bank robberies and murders led to an exhaustive eight-month investigation by the FBI.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1995, a mother of three was murdered and her corpse burned in Los Angeles, California. Agents hunted down a charismatic man named Glen Rogers who used charm and generosity to target women for his brutal attacks.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: When authorities in a small Washington town began investigating the disappearance of a young woman, they had no idea it would turn into a nationwide hunt for a sadistic sexual predator. The FBI was called in to help track down Darren Dee O'Neall.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: After an Arkansas state trooper is shot and killed, officers catch the murderer and alert the Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms and Explosives when they find weapons and explosives in the man's vehicle.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In July 1997, three people were shot to death at a Georgetown coffee shop. Investigators could find no immediate suspect or no clear motive for the murders. Was this a hate crime, a robbery gone wrong or an act of cold-blooded vengeance?

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1997, Jamie McMahan and Christopher Kauffman went on a crime spree of robbery, kidnapping and murder.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1980, a maid discovered that a copy machine, placed in a busy Las Vegas casino, was actually a bomb.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A cross-country crime spree over a Labor Day weekend terrorized the nation. In a small Ohio community, an elderly woman and teenage boy disappeared within hours of one another. The police suspected the cases were linked.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In the late 1980s, the FBI matched wits with a cunning bank robber known as the Shootist. He had performed more than 50 robberies with military precision and was responsible for the longest string of unsolved bank robberies in FBI history.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: After two brothers abduct and murder bank president Dan Short, FBI agents slowly piece together tiny shreds of evidence. Eventually, they're able to convict the Agofsky brothers for the 1989 Noel, Missouri bank robbery.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1993, a mother, her 8-year-old son and a live-in nurse were brutally murdered by an assassin. The murder-for-hire plot was meticulously planned and executed.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In September 1997, a team of masked gunmen robbed an armored car facility in Los Angeles and made off with nearly $19 million, the largest cash heist in U.S. history. FBI agents began conducting surveillance on a former employee of the facility.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Two robbers with assault rifles ambushed an armored car, killing both guards and seizing thousands of dollars.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: The bodies of several women began turning up around Vienna, Austria. The FBI and Austrian Federal Police investigated suspected serial killer Jack Unterweger, who became an international outlaw, hopping from country to country to evade capture.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1994, a rash of home invasions plagued the Detroit area. Each time, masked men disguised as police officers broke in with assault rifles and stole money and jewelry. But when one homeowner shot back, the identity of the intruders was revealed.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.sub-title: The True Story of Mississippi Burning
programme.desc: In 1964, civil rights workers who set out to educate Southern Black people about the right to vote disappeared in Mississippi. After they were found lynched, the FBI gained the trust of an informant who led to the conviction of local KKK members.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: An investigation into the man known as the 'Babes in the Wood Killer,' who evaded justice for thirty years.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Maria's marriage crumbled when Malcolm discovered her bigamy. As he moved on and found new love, Maria's jealousy erupted into dangerous rage, leading to a confrontation that exposed how far she was willing to go.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The story of the serial killer who brutally snatched away the lives of eight innocent women during a decade-long killing spree in Louisiana.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The chilling story of the serial killer who worked as a nurse in Texas, where she killed many children and infants by injecting them with drugs.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: In 2007, single mother of three Paige Birgfeld went missing. Police in Grand Junction, Colorado investigating her disappearance discovered she'd been secretly working as an escort and suspected one of her clients of abducting and killing her.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Teen Mhairi was killed on her walk home after a festive night. Investigators traced her last steps, using CCTV and forensics that pointed to Gavin, a violent repeat offender whose history revealed how dangerous he truly was.

programme.desc: Edward Johnson is sent to prison for the brutal murder of a female friend, but the violence doesn't end there.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: Remorseless murderer Casey Pigge's main ambition is to be the ultimate serial killer. If unrestrained, he remains a deadly threat.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: Convicted of shooting a man and 21 other felonies, Miti Maugaotega Jr. receives one of the longest prison sentences in Hawaiʻi history. Behind bars, he joins a prison gang and becomes a tornado of death and destruction.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The story of the serial killer who brutally snatched away the lives of eight innocent women during a decade-long killing spree in Louisiana.

programme.desc: The Bayou Strangler deceived, bound, assaulted and murdered men, as he drifted across South Louisiana.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: A fugitive from the United States tackles the British Police and goes on the run.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: A family's worst nightmare when 16-year-old Becky Watts goes missing from her home and is found dead a few days' later, murdered by her own stepbrother.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Teen Mhairi was killed on her walk home after a festive night. Investigators traced her last steps, using CCTV and forensics that pointed to Gavin, a violent repeat offender whose history revealed how dangerous he truly was.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: In October 2004, Rachel O'Reilly left her home to drop her children off at school. Less than 20 minutes after returning home she was murdered. Her husband, Joe O'Reilly, staged the crime scene to make it look like a burglary gone wrong.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Two young mothers vanished from East London's streets. At first unconnected, the cases came together when evidence found in Derek's home showed both women suffered the same violent fate at his hands.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The story of one of the most prolific serial killers in Canadian history, dubbed The Pig Farmer Killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Bruce Miller was shot to death in the office of a junkyard business. When a computer forensics expert examined the computers owned by the victim's wife, he found all the evidence needed.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The flu-like symptoms of a mother and her children proved to be something more serious: thallium poisoning.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A college student was found dead, and the evidence suggested he knew his killer. Three hairs and some microscopic cells helped police to unravel a web of lies, and find the motive for murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When the wife of a well-known dentist is found dead, police struggle to identify a suspect. Some fibers and a study of the weather patterns on the night of the murder break the case open.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Rachael Mullenix, 19, was convicted of stabbing her mother and then packing her bloodied corpse in a box.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1999, a 12-year-old was found murdered in her home in Minnesota. Despite a meticulous search of the home and an exhaustive investigation, police had no suspects.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Volunteers and police spend weeks searching for a missing girl. A psychic later leads them to a field where the body was found.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A 13-year-old girl went missing from her Colorado home and the only evidence the kidnapper left behind was three fingerprints on a window screen.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When Chris Ochoa confessed to the 1988 rape and murder of Nancy DePriest, the case was closed. 8 years later, Achim Marino confessed to the same crime. Can DNA evidence identify the real perpetrator?

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In a tragic twist of fate, just days after the woman sold her home and moved to a modest trailer, a fire took both the trailer and her life. But the autopsy proved this was no accident.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A police officer accused of killing his estranged wife insists she committed suicide. Investigators say it was murder - that it was physically impossible for the woman to have shot herself.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: After inspecting storm damage to a home in Tampa, an insurance assessor simply disappeared. Thirty hours later, her body was found in a nearby river. But the killer had been careless.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The police investigate when a severed leg is discovered among garbage. Conventional means of identifying the victim are impossible, leading investigators to rely on DNA, forensic anthropology and toxicology.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Alaskan hikers discover a body wrapped in sheets which were edged in orange stitching. Investigators find a tuft of red carpet fibers clinging to the sheet - threads of evidence which lead them straight to the killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When police discovered the body of Douglas Beamish's estranged wife, authorities linked 20 cat hairs found on her jacket to a cat owned by Beamish's parents. It was the first time animal DNA was used to solve a criminal case.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The wife of an Air Force officer was found dead in her bed with a plastic laundry bag near her face. Further investigation proved that the scene had been staged. Her death wasn't an accident, it was cold-blooded murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When Joann Katrinak is found dead in a corn field with her son, the obvious suspect is her husband. But insects found on the bodies reveal a vital clue which uncovers a twisted story.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a talented television news anchor is shot to death outside her home, it appears to be a crime of passion perpetrated by an obsessed fan.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 2008, the body of Colorado real estate developer Alan Helmick was found by his wife, Miriam, who then became the prime suspect.

programme.desc: When a hit-and-run boating accident caused a death, police must search for one boat among 1200 others.

programme.desc: It's usually easy to determine how a criminal entered the crime scene. But in this case, it was far from clear. It looked like the killer vanished into thin air...and perhaps he had.

programme.desc: A fan of murder mysteries and forensic science shows uses what she learned from TV to determine who was responsible for the accident which permanently injured her 4-year-old daughter.

programme.desc: When a young boy went missing, police organized the largest search in the history of their small town.

programme.desc: Police led an intense search and relied on forensics when the mother of two young children went missing.

programme.title: Forensic Files

programme.desc: When a decomposed body is found stuffed in a barrel, it's easy to presume murder. One of the few clues was an address book found along with the body.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Two men were murdered in their beds. The next night, arson destroyed a family planning clinic. Investigators find a link between the two separate crimes.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Heavy rains and snow run-off carried manure into Milwaukee's water supply in 1993, leading to an epidemic of gastrointestinal illness affecting 400,000 people.
